+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Repair invalid OpsAlert context strings in backend TypeScript files."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import re
+from pathlib import Path
+
+ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+BACKEND_SRC = ROOT / "backend" / "src"
+BAD_CONTEXT_RE = re.compile(
+    r"alertOnCriticalError\((?P<error>[^,]+),\s*'(?P<class>[A-Za-z0-9_]+)\.(?:Injectable|unknown|emit)'"
+)
+METHOD_RE = re.compile(
+    r"^\s*(?:private\s+|public\s+|protected\s+|static\s+|async\s+)*"
+    r"(?P<name>[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\("
+)
+SKIP = {"if", "for", "while", "switch", "catch", "constructor", "return", "throw"}
+
+
+def enclosing_method(lines: list[str], index: int) -> str:
+    for line in reversed(lines[: index + 1]):
+        match = METHOD_RE.match(line)
+        if match and match.group("name") not in SKIP and not line.lstrip().startswith("this."):
+            return match.group("name")
+    return "unknown"
+
+
+def repair(path: Path, write: bool) -> bool:
+    original = path.read_text()
+    lines = original.splitlines()
+    changed = False
+    output: list[str] = []
+    for index, line in enumerate(lines):
+        match = BAD_CONTEXT_RE.search(line)
+        if match:
+            method = enclosing_method(lines, index)
+            old = match.group(0)
+            new = old.rsplit(".", 1)[0] + f".{method}'"
+            line = line.replace(old, new)
+            changed = True
+        output.append(line)
+    if changed and write:
+        path.write_text("\n".join(output) + ("\n" if original.endswith("\n") else ""))
+    return changed
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
+    parser.add_argument("--write", action="store_true")
+    parser.add_argument("paths", nargs="*")
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+    paths = [ROOT / p for p in args.paths] if args.paths else BACKEND_SRC.rglob("*.ts")
+    changed = [p for p in paths if p.is_file() and repair(p, args.write)]
+    for path in changed:
+        print(path.relative_to(ROOT))
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Safely add OpsAlertService calls to logger error catch blocks."""

+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import os

+

+BACKEND_SRC = ROOT / "backend" / "src"
+CATCH_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*}\s*catch\s*\((?P<err>[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)(?::[^)]*)?\)\s*{")

+LOGGER_ERROR_RE = re.compile(r"this\.logger\.error\s*\(")
+METHOD_RE = re.compile(
+    r"^\s*(?:private\s+|public\s+|protected\s+|static\s+|async\s+)*"
+    r"(?P<name>[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\("
+)
+SKIP_METHODS = {"if", "for", "while", "switch", "catch", "constructor", "return", "throw"}
+
+
+def import_path(path: Path) -> str:
+    rel = os.path.relpath(BACKEND_SRC / "observability" / "ops-alert.service", path.parent)
+    rel = rel.replace(os.sep, "/")
+    return rel if rel.startswith(".") else f"./{rel}"
+
+
+def class_name(text: str) -> str | None:
+    match = CLASS_RE.search(text)
+    return match.group("name") if match else None
+
+
+def method_name(lines: list[str], index: int) -> str:
+    for line in reversed(lines[: index + 1]):
+        match = METHOD_RE.match(line)
+        if match and match.group("name") not in SKIP_METHODS and not line.lstrip().startswith("this."):
+            return match.group("name")
+    return "unknown"
+
+
+def ensure_imports(text: str, path: Path) -> str:
+    if "OpsAlertService" not in text:
+        lines = text.splitlines()
+        last_import = max((i for i, line in enumerate(lines) if line.startswith("import ")), default=-1)
+        lines.insert(last_import + 1, f"import {{ OpsAlertService }} from '{import_path(path)}';")
+        text = "\n".join(lines) + ("\n" if text.endswith("\n") else "")
+    if "Optional" not in text and "@nestjs/common" in text:
+        text = re.sub(
+            r"import\s*{([^}]*)}\s*from\s*'@nestjs/common';",
+            lambda m: "import {" + m.group(1).rstrip() + ", Optional } from '@nestjs/common';",
+            text,
+            count=1,
+        )
+    return text
+
+
+def ensure_constructor(text: str) -> str:
+    if "private readonly opsAlert" in text:
+        return text
+    constructor = re.search(r"constructor\s*\((?P<body>.*?)\)\s*{", text, re.S)
+    if not constructor:
+        return text
+    body = constructor.group("body").rstrip()
+    separator = "," if body.strip() else ""
+    replacement = f"constructor({body}{separator}\n    @Optional() private readonly opsAlert?: OpsAlertService,\n  ) {{"
+    return text[: constructor.start()] + replacement + text[constructor.end() :]
+
+
+def instrument(path: Path, write: bool) -> bool:
+    original = path.read_text()
+    cls = class_name(original)
+    if not cls or "opsAlert?.alertOnCriticalError" in original:
+        return False
+    lines = original.splitlines()
+    changed = False
+    output = list(lines)
+    offset = 0
+    for index, line in enumerate(lines):
+        catch = CATCH_RE.match(line)
+        if not catch:
+            continue
+        end = min(len(lines), index + 25)
+        logger_index = next((i for i in range(index + 1, end) if LOGGER_ERROR_RE.search(lines[i])), -1)
+        if logger_index < 0:
+            continue
+        indent = re.match(r"\s*", lines[logger_index]).group(0)

+            logger_index + 1 + offset,
+            f"{indent}void this.opsAlert?.alertOnCriticalError({catch.group('err')}, '{context}');",
+        )
+        offset += 1
+        changed = True
+    if changed:
+        text = ensure_constructor(ensure_imports("\n".join(output) + ("\n" if original.endswith("\n") else ""), path))
+        if write:
+            path.write_text(text)
+    return changed
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
+    parser.add_argument("--write", action="store_true")
+    parser.add_argument("paths", nargs="*")
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+    paths = [ROOT / p for p in args.paths] if args.paths else BACKEND_SRC.rglob("*.ts")
+    changed = [p for p in paths if p.is_file() and instrument(p, args.write)]
